features.summary: Goldman Sachs reported strong Q3 2021 results with net revenues of $13.6 billion and earnings per share of $14.93, driven by record performance in Investment Banking and Consumer & Wealth Management. Year-to-date revenues of nearly $47 billion and net earnings over $17.5 billion exceeded any full-year results in company history, generating ROE of nearly 26%. The firm announced two strategic acquisitions - NN Investment Partners ($320 billion AUS) and GreenSky - to accelerate growth in asset management and consumer banking while increasing recurring revenue streams.
features.management_outlook: Management remains optimistic about Goldman's growth trajectory despite increasing market uncertainties around inflation, COVID variants, U.S.-China relations, and political debates over economic policy. The firm continues executing its strategic evolution toward more durable earnings, with strong momentum in Investment Banking activity levels and Asset Management fundraising ($90 billion raised toward $150 billion goal). Management expects loan growth to accelerate in 2022 from pending acquisitions and maintains confidence in achieving sustainable mid-teens returns while building toward the consumer banking platform of the future.
features.risks: Key risks include potential headwinds from wage inflation trajectory, ongoing uncertainty around COVID Delta variant impacts, and U.S. political debates over infrastructure deals, debt ceiling extensions, and tax increases. The complex U.S.-China relationship remains a concern, and these factors collectively have potential to slow economic growth as indicated by downward GDP revisions. Additionally, the firm faces regulatory capital pressures with SACCR implementation expected to increase RWAs by $15 billion and reduce CET1 ratio by 30 basis points.
features.key_drivers: Performance was driven by elevated M&A activity with Goldman maintaining #1 league table position and 32% market share, strong equity underwriting with four consecutive quarters above $1 billion in revenues, and record Asset Management AUS of $1.7 trillion. Global Markets benefited from market share gains, heightened volatility, and strong commodities performance, while Consumer & Wealth Management achieved record results with 9 million customers on the multi-product platform. The firm's technology investments and cloud migration are enabling operational efficiencies and better client service delivery across all business segments.
